info.title: What virtual staging is, and why it sells
info.subtitle: A quick, honest rundown of how it works and who it's for — no jargon.

why.caption: Demoing Stagify at a Compass office in New York
why.title: Buyers shop with their eyes first
why.body: Almost every buyer starts online, so your listing photos are the real first showing. Empty or dated rooms are hard to read; people can't judge scale or picture living there. Staging gives each room a clear purpose and a pull, which is why agents have always relied on it.
why.p1: Empty rooms photograph smaller and colder than they are
why.p2: Staging shows the intended use of every space
why.p3: Stronger photos earn more clicks, saves, and showings

how.caption: Walking a room of agents through their first staged listing
how.title: From empty photo to listing-ready in about 8 seconds
how.body: Upload a photo, pick the room type and one of seven styles, and Stagify furnishes it in about eight seconds. Need changes? Remove the existing furniture, add a custom prompt, or mask one area to adjust it without starting over.
how.p1: 7 styles plus custom prompts and furniture removal
how.p2: Mask-edit any part of a result without redoing it
how.p3: Full copyright — use it on the MLS or in ads

who.caption: Sitting down one-on-one to stage a client's photos
who.title: Made for agents, sellers, and buyers
who.body: Agents market every listing in minutes instead of waiting on a staging crew. Sellers stage their own photos without hiring anyone. Buyers preview a renovation before they commit. And with AI Designer, you can turn CAD floor-plan PDFs into photorealistic 3D room renders.
who.p1: Agents: list sooner and close more
who.p2: Sellers & buyers: no software, no designer needed

team.caption: Training a brokerage team on Stagify
team.title: Easy enough for your whole office
team.body: There's no software to install and nothing to learn. Anyone on the team can open Stagify in a browser, upload a listing photo, and have it staged in seconds, whether on a laptop at the office or a phone in the field.
team.p1: No design skills or software needed
team.p2: Works in any browser, on any device
team.p3: Every agent can stage their own listings

compare.title: How it compares to traditional staging
compare.subtitle: Traditional staging works. It's just slow and expensive.

cost.trad: $2,000–$5,000+ per home, often billed monthly until it sells
cost.stag: Free to start — stage at no cost

time.trad: 1–3 weeks to schedule, deliver, and install

styles.stag: 7 styles plus custom prompts — switch anytime

changes.trad: New rental and install fees every time
changes.stag: Re-stage as many times as you like

logistics.trad: Movers, rentals, and access to the property
logistics.stag: Just upload a photo from anywhere

rights.stag: Yours to keep, with full copyright

whatIsVirtualStaging.question: What is virtual staging?
whatIsVirtualStaging.answer: Virtual staging is the process of digitally furnishing a property photo to showcase its potential, helping buyers envision how a space can look and feel when fully styled. Unlike traditional staging, it's faster, more cost-effective, and allows for multiple design options tailored to different buyer preferences. With high-quality visuals, virtual staging highlights the property's best features, making it easier for buyers to connect emotionally and imagine themselves living there.

howLong.question: How long does it take?
howLong.answer: On average, virtual staging takes around 8 seconds per photo to process, though the exact time can be a little shorter or longer depending on the complexity of the space and the design style chosen. The result is a quick, seamless transformation that makes your property photos stand out.

customTheme.question: How do I select a custom theme?
customTheme.answer: If you don't see the theme you want, you can select "Custom" and then add a specific prompt for the theme you want. We'll use your prompt to generate the theme you want.

idealRoom.question: What makes an ideal room for staging?
idealRoom.answer: An ideal room for staging should have good lighting, clear walls, and minimal clutter. Empty or sparsely furnished rooms work best, as they provide a clean canvas for the AI to add furniture and decor that matches your chosen style.

privacy.question: Is my data private and secure?
privacy.answer: Yes. We use HTTPS encryption, do not permanently store uploaded images after processing, and do not sell your personal information. You retain ownership of your uploads and generated results. We share data only with service providers needed to run Stagify (such as AI and payment processors) as described in our Privacy Policy.

contact.title: Get in touch

techHelp.metaDescription: Fix common technical issues with Stagify.ai. Solutions for uploads, generation, sign-in, billing, and browser problems.
techHelp.metaKeywords: stagify tech help, troubleshooting, upload not working, staging help, support, common issues
techHelp.title: Troubleshooting & common issues
techHelp.subtitle: Most problems have a quick fix. Find your issue below — and if you're still stuck, our team is one email away.

i1.a: Make sure the file is a JPG, PNG, or WEBP under 10 MB. Very large phone photos sometimes fail — resize the image or take a screenshot of it, then upload again. If it still fails, refresh the page and retry.

i2.a: Use a clear, well-lit photo taken straight-on, with the whole room visible and minimal clutter. Blurry, dark, or extreme wide-angle shots produce weaker results.

i1.q: My generation is stuck or taking too long.
i1.a: Most images finish in about 8 seconds. If it's been more than a minute, your connection may have dropped — refresh the page and try again. Avoid switching tabs or locking your phone while it runs.

i2.q: The result has odd furniture or artifacts.
i2.a: AI staging isn't perfect on every pass. Generate again for a new variation, switch to a different style, or use the masking tool to fix only the area that looks off instead of redoing the whole image.

i3.q: Nothing changed or the room still looks empty.
i3.a: This usually means a room type or style wasn't selected, or the photo was too dark for the model to read. Pick a room type and style, then try a brighter photo.

i1.a: Check your spam or promotions folder — codes can take a minute. If it still hasn't come, use "Resend code" and make sure your email is typed correctly.

i2.a: On the sign-in screen choose "Forgot your password?" and we'll email you a reset link. The link expires after one hour.

i3.a: Allow pop-ups for stagify.ai and make sure third-party cookies aren't fully blocked, then try again. You can also sign in with your email and password instead.

i1.q: How do I manage or cancel Stagify+?
i1.a: Click the profile icon in the top right, then tap the Stripe icon next to Stagify+ to open your billing dashboard, where you can update or cancel your subscription. You can also open the Stripe billing portal directly. You keep access until the end of the current billing period.

i2.q: I was charged but don't see Stagify+ features.
i2.a: Sign out and back in to refresh your account. If the features still don't appear, email us at team@stagify.ai from the address you used.

i1.a: Use an up-to-date version of Chrome, Safari, Edge, or Firefox. Older browsers may not support the editor or the drag-to-compare tools.

i2.q: The page is slow or buttons don't respond.
i2.a: Hard-refresh the page (Ctrl/Cmd + Shift + R) to clear a stale version, close unused tabs, and disable ad blockers for stagify.ai, which can interfere with uploads.

i3.q: The site won't load at all.
i3.a: Check your internet connection, then try a private/incognito window. If it loads there, clear your browser cache for stagify.ai.

contact.text: Still stuck? Our team is happy to help. Email
